blocks:
Ayahs

هَلْ أَتَىٰكَ حَدِيثُ ٱلْغَـٰشِيَةِ (۱)
Did you receive the account of the Enveloper? (1)
وُجُوهٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ خَـٰشِعَةٌ (۲)
Some faces on that day will be humbled, (2)
عَامِلَةٌ نَّاصِبَةٌ (۳)
wrought-up and weary: (3)
تَصْلَىٰ نَارًا حَامِيَةً (٤)
they will enter a scorching fire (4)
تُسْقَىٰ مِنْ عَيْنٍ ءَانِيَةٍ (٥)
and made to drink from a boiling spring. (5)
لَّيْسَ لَهُمْ طَعَامٌ إِلَّا مِن ضَرِيعٍ (٦)
They will have no food except cactus, (6)
لَّا يُسْمِنُ وَلَا يُغْنِى مِن جُوعٍ (۷)
neither nourishing, nor of avail against hunger. (7)

Key Points

| «Ghāshiyah» means "the Overwhelming" or "the Enveloper," and what is meant by it is the event of the Resurrection (Qiyamah) which covers everyone. «‘Āmilah» means "the striving" and «Nāṣibah» means "arduous labor" which exhausts a person. «Ḥāmiyah» means "extremely hot" and «Āniyah» is the ultimate degree of burning. It is possible that the intent of «wujūh» (faces) is the owners of those faces and prominent figures, meaning the distinguished figures of each of the two groups: the good and the bad. «Ḍarī‘», according to the statement of the Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, is something in Hell similar to a thorn, which is fouler smelling than carrion, hotter than fire, and extremely bitter. (26) Perhaps the naming of this thorn as «Ḍarī‘» is because eating it compels a person to cry out and supplicate (taḍarru‘). Much arduous work is performed in this world, but because it lacks sincerity, or was not done according to Divine command, or was accompanied by self-admiration, pride, obligation (mann), sin, or polytheism (shirk), it becomes nullified (habṭ), wasted, and will not yield benefit on the Day of Resurrection. In the Divine worldview, the element of work must be aligned and sacred alongside the element of goal and direction, and the element of leadership.

26) Tafsir Majma‘ al-Bayān.

Messages

1- The event of the Resurrection is so comprehensive that everyone must be mindful of it. «هل اتاك حدیث الغاشیة»

2- Not every effort and hardship will be beneficial on the Day of Resurrection. «عاملة ناصبة»

3- Only the sincere group will benefit from their efforts. «لسعیها راضیة»

4- The Resurrection is physical (bodily), because the issue of gaining weight or losing weight is only applicable to physical bodies. «لایُسمن»

Explanations

| Portrait of Surah Al-Ghashiyah This Surah has twenty-six verses and was revealed in Mecca. «Ghāshiyah» is one of the names of the Resurrection (Qiyamah), which appears in the first verse of this Surah and is also the name of the Surah. Like other Meccan Surahs, this Surah addresses the states of the people of Paradise and the people of Hellfire on the Day of Resurrection, serving to warn the polytheists and give glad tidings to the believers. Then, signs of Allah’s power in the creation of the earth, the heavens, and certain creatures are mentioned, which serve both as an argument for the Oneness of the Originator of existence and as proof of Allah’s power to recreate human beings on the Day of Resurrection. And finally, He commands His Prophet to constantly remind and guide the people, and of course, to know that not everyone is destined to believe in him, for Allah has willed that human beings choose their path through their own free will.

Ayahs

وُجُوهٌ يَوْمَئِذٍ نَّاعِمَةٌ (۸)
Some faces on that day will be joyous, (8)
لِّسَعْيِهَا رَاضِيَةٌ (۹)
pleased with their endeavour, (9)
فِى جَنَّةٍ عَالِيَةٍ (۱٠)
in a lofty garden, (10)
لَّا تَسْمَعُ فِيهَا لَـٰغِيَةً (۱۱)
where they will not hear any vain talk. (11)

Key Points

The term «ناعمة» means living in comfort and bliss, and «لاغیة» means vain or useless speech.

Messages

1- True joy and delight are reflected on the face. «وجوه... ناعمة» (Faces... blissful). The color of the countenance reveals the secret of the heart.

2- The blessings of Paradise and the special Divine favors are the results of effort and striving. «ناعمة لسعیها» (blissful because of their striving).

3- The hardship of work in this world is fleeting, but it brings permanent joy and success in the Hereafter. «لسعیها راضیة» (pleased with their striving).

4- Paradise is the pinnacle of material and spiritual elevation. «جنّة عالیة» (a lofty Garden).

5- Those who avoid vanity (laghw) in this world will live in an environment free of vanity in the next abode. «لا تسمع فیها لاغیة» (wherein they shall hear no vanity).

6- In Paradise, not only is there no vanity (laghw), but there is also no speech that could in any way lead to the creation of vanity. «لا تسمع فیها لاغیة» (wherein they shall hear no vanity).

Ayahs

فِيهَا عَيْنٌ جَارِيَةٌ (۱۲)
In it is a flowing spring (12)
فِيهَا سُرُرٌ مَّرْفُوعَةٌ (۱۳)
and raised couches, (13)
وَأَكْوَابٌ مَّوْضُوعَةٌ (۱٤)
with goblets set, (14)
وَنَمَارِقُ مَصْفُوفَةٌ (۱٥)
and cushions laid out in an array, (15)
وَزَرَابِىُّ مَبْثُوثَةٌ (۱٦)
and carpets spread out. (16)

Key Points

The word "عین" (Ayn), meaning spring or fountain, can be applied to one or several springs, and based on Ayah 15 of Surah Adh-Dhariyat, the springs of Paradise are numerous. «ان المتقین فى جنّات و عیون» "سُرر" (Surur) and "سرور" (Surūr - joy/delight) share the same root. A sarīr is a couch or throne designated for delight. The word "مرفوعة" (marfū'ah) indicates the elevated position of the couches of Paradise, which overlook the scenic views of Paradise. "اكواب" (Akwāb) is the plural of "كوب" (Kūb), which is pronounced like khūb (good) and means a goblet or cup without a handle. In Surah Al-Waqi'ah, Ayahs 17 and 18, various types of vessels are mentioned. «باكواب و اباریق و كأس من معین» "نمارق" (Namāriq) is the plural of "نمرقة" (Numruqah), meaning a small cushion or pillow (for leaning on). "زرابىّ" (Zarābiyy) refers to luxurious, gold-woven carpets.

Messages

1- The grandeur of the springs of Paradise is beyond comprehension. «فیها عین جاریة» (The word "عین" is used in the indefinite form [nakirah]).

2- The station of the people of Paradise is lofty and elevated; they do not sit on the ground. «سُرر مرفوعة»

Ayahs

أَفَلَا يَنظُرُونَ إِلَى ٱلْإِبِلِ كَيْفَ خُلِقَتْ (۱۷)
Do they not observe the camel, [to see] how it has been created? (17)
وَإِلَى ٱلسَّمَآءِ كَيْفَ رُفِعَتْ (۱۸)
and the sky, how it has been raised? (18)
وَإِلَى ٱلْجِبَالِ كَيْفَ نُصِبَتْ (۱۹)
and the mountains, how they have been set? (19)
وَإِلَى ٱلْأَرْضِ كَيْفَ سُطِحَتْ (۲٠)
and the earth, how it has been surfaced? (20)

Key Points

If the spirit of contemplation exists in man, the entire universe is a classroom. Even a shepherd in the desert is, as it were, in the world's largest library; beneath his feet is the earth, above his head is the sky, around him are the mountains, and before him are the camels. If he pays close attention to each, he will discover many secrets. For example, the camel runs faster than the horse and carries more load than the donkey. Among animals, some are used for riding, some for meat, and some for milk, but every part of the camel is useful. Its eyelids are resistant to the wind and dust of the desert. It stores fat and food in its hump, making it resistant to hunger. It retains water in its body, making it resistant to thirst. It knows the way. It is so docile that hundreds of camels submit to a single camel driver. The sole of its foot was created for the sandy terrain. Its neck serves as a step for mounting and a lever for lifting loads. In short, as the Arabs say, the camel is the ship of the desert. It is the strongest, least costly, most beneficial, calmest, and most patient animal. The creation of the heavens, the mountains, and the earth is not accidental; rather, they were created with perfect wisdom and placed in their proper positions. In every blessing, one aspect stands out (in the camel, the manner of creation; in the sky, its height; in the earth, its vastness; and in the mountains, the manner of their installation). The heavens, the planets, their orbits, their order, and their calculation—the wonders of which are discovered daily—are perpetual fields for study, discovery, and contemplation. The mountains and their roots deep within the earth, like pegs and chain links, protect the earth from the shaking and instability caused by the molten materials inside the earth. They store snow and gradually release it, block storms, and help purify the air. They are reservoirs for various minerals and industrial materials, serve as landmarks for travelers, and provide a bed for the growth of certain plants. Furthermore, mountains, through various precious and ornamental stones like agate, or construction stones like marble used in buildings, are an endless resource for humanity. In summary, these verses somehow refer to issues of cosmology and even all matters such as agriculture, animal husbandry, industry, and spatial affairs.

Messages

1- Those who do not delve deeply into discovering the realities of existence are subject to Allah's reproach. «افلا ینظرون»

2- Pass beyond the perceptible to reach the intelligible. «افلا ینظرون...»

3- Do not doubt the Resurrection (Ma'ad) and the special blessings of the people of Paradise. Do you not see Allah's wondrous creations in this world? «افلا ینظرون الى الابل»

4- Just as the existence of creations proves the existence of Allah, the manner and complexity of the creations also prove His power, knowledge, and wisdom. «كیف، كیف، كیف...»

Ayahs

فَذَكِّرْ إِنَّمَآ أَنتَ مُذَكِّرٌ (۲۱)
So admonish—for you are only an admonisher, (21)
لَّسْتَ عَلَيْهِم بِمُصَيْطِرٍ (۲۲)
and not a taskmaster over them— (22)
إِلَّا مَن تَوَلَّىٰ وَكَفَرَ (۲۳)
except him who turns back and disbelieves. (23)
فَيُعَذِّبُهُ ٱللَّهُ ٱلْعَذَابَ ٱلْأَكْبَرَ (۲٤)
Him Allah will punish with the greatest punishment. (24)
إِنَّ إِلَيْنَآ إِيَابَهُمْ (۲٥)
Indeed to Us will be their return. (25)
ثُمَّ إِنَّ عَلَيْنَا حِسَابَهُم (۲٦)
Then, indeed, their reckoning will lie with Us. (26)

Key Points

Reminder (or admonition) is for the completion of the proof (Hujjah), so that the disbelievers do not say on the Day of Judgment: «لولا ارسلت الینا رسولاً فنتّبع»(27) O God! Why did You not send us a warner, so that we might follow him? Of course, none but the people of intellect take heed from these reminders. «انّما یتذكّر اولوا الالباب»(28)

In the previous Surah (Surah Al-A'la), Allah commanded His Prophet to remind, saying: You remind, for whoever fears (Allah) will take heed, and the wretched ones will avoid the reminder and will be afflicted by the greatest punishment. «یتجنّبها الاشقى الّذى یصلى النّار الكبرى» In this Surah too, He says: You remind, but know that a group will turn away and will be afflicted by the greater punishment. «الاّ من تولّى و كَفَر فَیُعذّبه اللّه العذاب الاكبر» In any case, the «نار كبرى» (Greater Fire) in Surah Al-A'la and the «عذاب اكبر» (Greater Punishment) in this Surah are the retribution for those who do not accept the reminder and ignore the divine counsel and admonition.

Musaytir (مُصیطر), derived from satr and saytarah (dominion/control), refers to someone who subjugates those under his command and organizes them in the direction he desires.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: «كلّ امّة یحاسبها امام زمانها و یعرف الائمة اولیائهم و اعدائهم بسیماهم»(29) (The account of every nation is the responsibility of the Imam of their time, and the Imams recognize their friends and enemies by their features.) The account of every nation is the responsibility of the Imam of their time, and the Imams recognize their friends and enemies by their features, just as it is stated in Surah Al-A'raf: «و على الاعراف رجال یعرفون كلا بسیماهم»(30) In Ziyarat Jami'ah, addressing the Infallibles علیهم السلام, we also read: «و ایاب الخلق الیكم و حسابهم علیكم»(31) (The return of creation is to you, and their reckoning is upon you.) This meaning has also been narrated from Imam Kadhim, Imam Baqir, and Imam Hadi علیهم السلام.(32)

The phrase «الاّ من تولّى و كفر» is an exception to the phrase «انّما انت مذكّر». Meaning: O Prophet! You are only a reminder, except for him who turns away and disbelieves, in which case your reminder will have no effect on him.

Imam Baqir علیه السلام said: The intent of the Greater Punishment (al-'Adhab al-Akbar) is the severe and eternal punishment.(33)

27) Taha, 134. 28) Ra'd, 19. 29) Bihar, vol. 8, p. 339. 30) Al-A'raf, 46. 31) Mafatih al-Jinan. 32) Tafsir al-Mizan. 33) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn.

Messages

  1. The Prophet is commissioned with the duty, not the result. «انّما انت مُذكّر» (The Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله was eager and keen for the guidance of the people, and Allah told him: Your duty is only to remind.)
  2. The duty of the prophets is to remind, not to dominate or impose belief. «لَسْت علیهم بمصیطر»
  3. The indifference of the people does not negate the duty. «انما انت مذكّر لَست علیهم بمصیطر»
  4. The source of disbelief (Kufr) is obstinacy, stubbornness, and turning away from truth and reality. «من تولّى و كفر»
  5. Allah's wrath has degrees. «العذاب الاكبر»
  6. Escape from the Divine wrath is impossible for anyone. «انّ الینا ایابهم»
  7. Reckoning is among the traditions that Allah has made incumbent upon Himself. «انّ علینا حسابهم»
